name: Pesto AI
description: Pesto AI, founded in 2017 by Ayush Jaiswal and Swiggy co-founder Rahul Jaimini, built an AI-driven platform to help companies recruit remote developers, primarily connecting Indian engineering talent with global opportunities. After raising over $8M from investors including Matrix Partners, Ryan Hoover, and Sahil Lavingia, the team was acqui-hired by Scale AI in June 2025. Pesto shut down its independent operations, with founder Jaiswal becoming head of growth at Scale.
story: Pesto AI launched in 2017 with a straightforward pitch — use AI to match Indian software developers with remote jobs at global companies. Co-founded by Ayush Jaiswal and Swiggy co-founder Rahul Jaimini, the platform raised over $8 million from investors including Matrix Partners, ProductHunt creator Ryan Hoover, and Gumroad founder Sahil Lavingia. The company built a 71-person operation and carved out a niche in the Indian developer talent marketplace. But the recruiting industry proved brutal for AI-first startups: margins were thin, enterprise sales cycles were long, and larger platforms with deeper pockets kept encroaching. In June 2025, Scale AI — the data-labeling giant flush with defense contracts and a $13.8 billion valuation — acqui-hired the Pesto team. Founder Jaiswal became head of growth at Scale, and Pesto's independent operations ceased. The product died, but the team landed softly — a better outcome than most on this list.

sources.title: Scale AI hires team behind remote developer recruiting platform Pesto AI
